extraction.one_line_summary: BUCHMANN Kimmel won from a 6-rider breakaway at 2km that survived despite only 2-minute gap and 46kph pace, attacking 300m from line.
tactical_patterns: Six-rider break survived with 2-minute gap at 30km thanks to tailwind pushing 46kph average, losing only 10sec/km.
Cofidis deployed dual sprinter leadout (FDJ-Nasser-Bora) 30km out despite 2-minute deficit, revealing coordination failure between sprint teams.
BUCHMANN attacked 300m out despite being marked as non-fastest finisher, capitalizing on break exhaustion and surprise timing.

rider_signals.BUCHMANN Kimmel: positive: Yellow jersey holder animated early 2km break, cleared all climbs first for points, still had finishing kick after 170km
rider_signals.HOULE Hugo: neutral: 85th overall but best-placed escapee suggests weak GC field allowed low-ranked rider in dangerous move
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: Won peloton sprint but failed to close 2-minute gap with organized chase, revealing sprint train coordination issues
